commit.message:
Fix {{discount}} interpolation using matched product's actual savings

When discount_percent is 0 from the backend, the SDK was looking up
savings from eligible.first which may not be the user's actual product.
Now defers discount resolution to MigrationManager where the matched
product is known, and re-interpolates {{discount}} with the real
web vs StoreKit price savings percentage.

commit.message:
Root cause found: TT contention in dock hub, not xHCI controller

The USB spec guarantees interrupt bandwidth over bulk at the xHCI level,
but the dock's internal hub Transaction Translator is the real bottleneck.
Best fix: plug mouse into a motherboard port to bypass the shared TT.

- README: lead with hardware fix, keep watchdog as fallback
- Watchdog v3.2: faster recovery (4s silence, 2 confirms at 1s = ~5s)
- Add kernel-level research notes (KERNEL-FIX-RESEARCH.md)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Align Brain email context limit to 10 across web and Telegram

Both the shared processBrainChat and the web chat route had
MAX_CONTEXT_EMAILS set to 8, causing the AI to see fewer emails
than the Telegram /emails command which fetched 10.

commit.message:
Enrich delinquent accounts workflow with DC-specific legislation and procedures

All three approaches now reference actual DC Code provisions and procedures:

Pre-Legal (9 steps): Full board-managed collection workflow grounded in
DC Code § 42-1903.12 (late fees, collection costs) and § 42-1903.13
(assessment liens, super-lien priority). Includes ledger verification,
certified mail notices, formal demand with 30-day cure, payment plans,
board lien resolution, lien recording at DC Recorder of Deeds, and
super-lien notification to first mortgage holder.

Self-Represented (6 steps): Actionable lien-to-collection process
including recording liens at DC Recorder of Deeds, notifying mortgage
holders of 6-month super-priority under § 42-1903.13(a)(2), filing in
DC Superior Court Small Claims Branch ($10K limit), obtaining and
recording judgments, payment plan negotiation, and lien release.

Legal Counsel (5 steps): Attorney-led collection from demand through
foreclosure, including super-lien enforcement, DC Superior Court
litigation, lien foreclosure procedures, and guidance on bankruptcy,
estate/probate, and FHA/VA complications.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1 Edit Bridge — replace compute_edit with loom TextDelta API (#15)

* fix: adapt crdt module to event-graph-walker api-hardening

- Remove @core/@causal_graph imports (now internal packages)
- Replace merge_remote(ops, frontier) with apply_sync(SyncMessage)
- Add export_all/export_since/apply_sync/get_version to Editor and
  ParsedEditor using the new SyncSession-based sync protocol
- Add SyncMessage::to_json_string/from_json_string for JS FFI
- Add Version::to_json_string/from_json_string for export_since_json
- Overhaul crdt.mbt JS exports: replace *_json frontier/ops functions
  with export_all_json, export_since_json, apply_sync_json, get_version_json
- Fix undo_manager_undo/redo return type: String -> Bool
- Update all tests to use new SyncMessage-based sync API
- Fix cmd/main: remove @core usage in demo.mbt and repl.mbt

All 168 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(editor): add edit_bridge using loom TextDelta API

merge_to_edits(old, new) replaces bespoke compute_edit by delegating
to loom's text_to_delta → to_edits pipeline. Parity tests confirm
identical Edit values for all single-change cases in text_diff_test.mbt.

Refs: docs/design/01-edit-bridge.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(editor): wire ParsedEditor.reparse to edit_bridge

Replace three-way if / compute_edit with merge_to_edits from the
Edit Bridge. ParsedEditor now uses loom's TextDelta pipeline for
its string-diff fallback path. All existing tests pass unchanged.

Refs: docs/design/01-edit-bridge.md, docs/design/02-reactive-pipeline.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(editor): regenerate .mbti and format after edit_bridge

* fix(ci): resolve all moon check --deny-warn failures

- Push SyncMessage/Version JSON methods to event-graph-walker submodule
- Update submodule pointer to include new JSON serialization methods
- Remove unused @ast import from root moon.pkg
- Fix unused 'model' variable in projection test (rename to _model)
- Suppress warn-list -2-6 in projection/moon.pkg for intentional
  future-use enum constructors (unused_value, unused_constructor)

* fix(ci): replace parser with loom/loom in test-submodules matrix

The parser submodule was replaced by loom. Update the CI matrix to
test loom/loom (the main loom module) instead of the non-existent
parser directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(editor): simplify reparse() by removing dead multi-edit loop

text_to_delta always produces at most one contiguous change block
[Retain(n), Delete(m), Insert(s)], so to_edits returns 0 or 1 Edits.
The multi-edit loop was unreachable code. Replace it with a direct
single-edit call and add an explanatory comment.

paths for build-js and web-test jobs.

commit.message:
feat(cashflow): match Excel expense timing for fixed monthly costs

Software, agency, accounting, and insurance now hit as monthly lumps
(week containing the 1st) instead of weekly spread. Matches the Excel
cash flow model timing. Seed defaults updated to monthly amounts:
- Software: $10,541/mo
- Agency: $7,000/mo
- Accounting: $2,500/mo
- Insurance: $3,000/mo

Uses trailing monthly total from bank data when available, falls back
to seed defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: provider-aware token estimation and oversized chunk prevention (#72) (#72)

CharsPerToken=3.5 was correct for Ollama but too permissive for LiteLLM/OpenAI
(tiktoken ≈1.3 chars/token), allowing chunks up to 26k chars through when the
actual safe limit is ~11k. Additionally, the AST chunker appended oversized
parent chunks before recursing into children, so they were never removed.

- Add WithRatio variants for token estimation (1.5 for LiteLLM, 3.5 for Ollama)
- Fix walkTree to skip oversized parents instead of appending then recursing
- Add pre-embed truncation guard in pipeline for gap/fallback chunks
- Wire provider-aware ratio through indexer → chunker and indexer → pipeline

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rename TicketDeflection to PRDtoProd (#378)

## Summary

- Rename the .NET project from `TicketDeflection` to `PRDtoProd` to
align codebase identity with what it actually is
- Update namespaces in ~95 `.cs` files, 4 `.cshtml` Razor pages, EF Core
migration snapshots
- Update all infrastructure: Dockerfile, 4 workflows,
`autonomy-policy.yml` (18 glob patterns), `AGENTS.md`, 6 scripts, 3 test
scripts
- Fix pre-existing test assertion mismatch in
`test-check-autonomy-policy.sh`
- Domain concepts (`TicketDbContext`, `Ticket` model, etc.) deliberately
preserved

## Test plan

- [x] `dotnet build PRDtoProd.sln` — 0 errors, 0 warnings
- [x] `dotnet test PRDtoProd.sln` — 168/168 passing
- [x] `bash scripts/tests/test-run-lifecycle-lib.sh` — passed
- [x] `bash scripts/tests/test-check-autonomy-policy.sh` — passed
- [x] `bash scripts/tests/test-demo-preflight.sh` — passed
- [ ] CI passes on this PR
- [ ] Verify no stale `TicketDeflection` references in active code paths

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Improve gyro/tilt controls: tuning, response curve, calibration, orientation API (#75)

* Tune gyro constants and wire BALANCE_DEFAULTS into input-manager

- Reduce sensitivity 40→25° for comfortable tilt range
- Increase deadzone 2→4° to prevent pedal-tap jitter
- Tighten low-pass filter 0.3→0.1 to reduce noise
- Replace hardcoded values in input-manager with config references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add response curve and output smoothing to tilt controls

- Add responseCurve (1.8) power exponent for fine center control
- Add outputSmoothing (0.25) EMA for smooth lean transitions
- Fix normalization to use (sensitivity - deadzone) for full ±1.0 range
- Dead zone now smoothly decays toward 0 instead of snapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add delayed calibration via sample averaging

- Collect 10 samples before setting motionOffset (averages out noise)
- No steering output during calibration (~170ms collection window)
- Replace all direct motionOffset assignments with startTiltCalibration()
- Gauge-tap, game reset, and recalibrate all use averaged calibration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use DeviceOrientation as primary API + auto-recalibrate on resume

- Swap API priority: deviceorientation is primary (browser sensor fusion),
  devicemotion is fallback only if orientation events don't fire
- Request DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission() on iOS (required)
- Auto-recalibrate when re-enabling motion in lobby toggle
- Replace _useAccel flag with _useOrientation (inverted logic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix iPhone motion detection and align test/input.html with game code

Fix: _startMotionListening() could be called twice during iOS permission
flow (orientation grant + motion grant), adding duplicate event listeners
and breaking motion detection. Added guard to prevent re-entry.

Align test/input.html with game implementation:
- Update slider defaults to match BALANCE_DEFAULTS (sensitivity 25,
  deadzone 4, lowPassK 0.1)
- Add response curve and output smoothing sliders + logic
- Add sample-averaged calibration (calibSamples slider)
- Swap to orientation-first API priority with motion fallback
- Request DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission() on iOS
- Guard startMotionListening() against duplicate calls
- Update WebHID gyro lean to use response curve

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix iOS motion: request DeviceMotionEvent permission first

On iOS, DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission() grants access to both
motion and orientation events. Calling DeviceOrientationEvent permission
first consumed the user gesture context, causing the motion permission
to fail — and on some iOS versions, orientation-only permission doesn't
reliably enable deviceorientation events.

Fix: call DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission() first (proven iOS API),
then fall back to DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission() only if
motion API isn't available. Listener priority remains orientation-first.

Also align test/input.html with the same fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix calibration buffer reset that prevented motion from ever working

Bug: _applyTilt() called startTiltCalibration() on every sensor event
when motionOffset was null, which reset _calibBuf = [] each time. The
buffer never accumulated 10 samples, so calibration never completed
and motionLean stayed at 0 forever.

Fix: only call startTiltCalibration() if not already calibrating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add 3D arch HUD to test page, fix diagnostics, align defaults

- Import and use the game's ArchIndicator (3D Three.js) in test/input.html
- Arch appears when Motion or Gamepad+Gyro source is active, shows lean needle
- Fix diagnostics referencing stale _useAccel → _useOrientation
- Add responseCurve, outputSmoothing, calibSamples sliders to tuning panel
- Slider defaults match BALANCE_DEFAULTS: sensitivity 25, deadzone 4, lowPassK 0.1
- Expose input and inputSource on window for cross-script access

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Separate tuning for mobile tilt vs controller gyro

Mobile tilt (DeviceOrientation) provides absolute angles from browser
sensor fusion — smooth, bounded signal. Controller gyro (WebHID)
integrates angular velocity — faster accumulation, more volatile.
Using the same tuning for both produced different feel characteristics.

Mobile tilt tuning:
- sensitivity: 25° (unchanged)
- responseCurve: 1.8 → 1.4 (less aggressive, fixes slow→sudden feel)
- outputSmoothing: 0.25 (unchanged)

Controller gyro tuning (new separate values):
- gyroSensitivity: 40° (wider range since accumulation is faster)
- gyroDeadzone: 4°
- gyroResponseCurve: 1.3 (gentler curve for integrated signal)
- gyroOutputSmoothing: 0.3 (more smoothing for noisier signal)

_applyTilt() now accepts isGyro flag to select appropriate tuning.
Test page updated with separate slider groups for each input type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add checkpoint restart counter to victory screen stats

Track how many times the player restarts from a checkpoint during a race
and display the count (🏁 Restarts) on the victory screen when > 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add gyro improvements: accel drift correction, L3 recenter, sensitivity slider, frame-rate independent filter

- Replace blanket gyro drift decay with accelerometer-assisted correction using
  gravity vector (atan2) for physically grounded drift fix
- Add recenterGyro() for instant L3 button re-zero (400ms flash vs 800ms full recalibrate)
- Add sensitivity slider (10-45°) with localStorage persistence, shown when motion active
- Make low-pass filter frame-rate independent using event.interval
- Export mutable TUNE object from config.js; bike-model, arch-indicator, and
  input-manager now read from TUNE for runtime adjustability
- Add accel diagnostics and gyroAccelCorrection tuning slider to test page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* make sensitivity slider visible with flex

* Change Perfect Game to No Crashes

* Fix motion controls v3 review issues: dynamic arch, scaled deadzone, accel guard

- Arch indicator rebuilds when sensitivity slider changes (was using stale
  module-level constants computed once at import time)
- Deadzone scales proportionally with sensitivity (~16% tilt, ~10% gyro)
  so extreme settings remain playable
- Accel byte offsets verified via gravity magnitude check before using
  accelerometer drift correction; falls back to blanket decay if invalid
- recenterGyro() only zeros gyro accumulator, no longer hard-resets shared
  smoothing state that could glitch mobile tilt
- Remove unused BALANCE_DEFAULTS import from input-manager
- Document why _resetGame uses tilt calibration only (not gyro)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make sensitivity slider vertical on right edge with larger thumb

commit.message:
Bundle claude-agent-sdk into worker instead of externalizing it

The SDK is pure JS (no native .node binaries), so it can be bundled
directly into worker-service.cjs. This eliminates the CJS/ESM
mismatch that caused "Cannot find module" errors on remote installs,
and removes the need for node_modules in the plugin cache.

Reverts the CJS→ESM approach from f535c54 in favor of this simpler fix.

- Provide a concise, implementation-ready reference that explains why
model autonomy requires a coordination/control plane (MCP) rather than
relying on raw LLM outputs.
- Capture minimal, actionable architecture for Spec → DAG → coordinated
agents → verification to address recurring questions about “models
shipping themselves”.

### Description
- Adds `docs/MCP_ORCHESTRATION_STACK_MINIMAL.md` with a minimal MCP
design covering spec parsing, work-graph/DAG building,
scheduling/concurrency control, blocker escalation, shared state,
verification gates, MAESTRO alignment, and a rollout plan.

`revision_note`, and appending a completed initiative entry
`mcp-orchestration-stack-minimal`.

was created with metadata including `change_classification: patch` and
JSON validation evidence.

succeeded.

docs/MCP_ORCHESTRATION_STACK_MINIMAL.md docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to
verify intended changes.
- Committed staged files via `git commit` and confirmed the new file and
roadmap update were added to the repo successfully.

multi-page polish pass (#81)

* fix custom domain routing with postbuild config injection

vercel.json rewrites run after filesystem, so nbanow.app/showswipe.app
always served index.html. postbuild script injects host-based route
rules before the filesystem handler in vercel's build output config,
so custom domains correctly rewrite to their app pages.

commit.message:
Fix correctness issues from multi-model review

- Fix offset bug: pid * tl.arange -> pid * BLOCK + tl.arange (gpu-kernel-optimization)
- Fix FlashAttention: replace tensor if with unconditional tl.maximum update,
  remove deprecated trans_b kwarg in favor of tl.trans()
- Fix divmod (not supported in Triton JIT) -> // and % (persistent-warp-matmul)
- Unify dropout: seed-based Philox as default in epilogue file, matching
  memory-efficient-patterns guidance
- Add portability tier headers (Targets: Triton version, GPU arch) to all files
- Soften power-of-two rule to "strongly preferred" not "must"
- Mark router skill examples as verified (from official tutorials)

fix(python): use fully qualified imports for walk_forward, shap_analysis, calibration

Bare imports (e.g., `from walk_forward import ...`) fail when running
as `python.server.main` from /app. Changed to `from python.server.walk_forward import ...`
to match existing train_models import pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add benchmark system for evaluating prompts across QMSum meetings

Implements a full benchmark pipeline at /benchmark that:
- Loads QMSum meeting transcripts from GitHub (20 curated meetings)
- Runs the generator model (GPT-OSS-120B via Groq) across configurable
  meetings x context windows x prompts
- Judges outputs using OpenAI GPT-5.2 with a modular rubric (trigger,
  card, and set judges with 8-dimension scoring)
- Displays comparison results in a drill-down table with JSON/CSV export

Key architecture decisions:
- Headless tick runner reuses existing fillPrompt/formatTranscriptWindow
- Judge runs trigger + card evaluations in parallel within each tick
- 10 ticks judged concurrently for throughput
- Deterministic validation (word count, char count) runs before LLM judge
- Composite scoring with weighted formulas for trigger/card/set blocks

- Added modal dismissal (Escape key + close button) before tab clicks in study detail tests — changelog modal was intercepting pointer events
- Fixed API 404 fallback: Route::fallback() returns JSON 404 (was returning 500 for unknown routes)

commit.message:
fix: corregir fórmula nextMatchNumber en generación de llaves de eliminación simple

La fórmula anterior hacía que todos los partidos de una ronda apuntaran al
mismo nextMatchNumber, causando que solo el primer match de la siguiente ronda
recibiera equipos y los demás quedaran TBD.

Fix: nextMatchNumber += Math.floor(i / 2) para distribuir correctamente
los ganadores en pares (1y2→A, 3y4→B, 5y6→C, 7y8→D).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
